summary: AgentForge v4.5 Final Testing - All features working. 22/22 backend tests passed (100%). Frontend 3D Map using pure Three.js works without R3F errors. BuildFarmPanel with real-time polling working correctly.

issues: View mode selector buttons (radial, spiral, cluster, tree) visible but icon-only. Could add tooltips for clarity

passed_tests: ✅ GET /api/ - API root returns version 4.5.0 with build_farm feature
✅ GET /api/health - health check returns 'healthy'
✅ GET /api/build-farm/status - returns real-time status (total_workers, active_workers, queued_jobs, etc)
✅ GET /api/build-farm/workers - returns 3 workers list
✅ POST /api/build-farm/jobs/add - adds job to queue with status='queued'
✅ GET /api/build-farm/jobs - returns jobs list sorted by priority
✅ GET /api/build-farm/jobs/{id}/logs - returns 404 for nonexistent job
✅ POST /api/build-farm/jobs/{id}/cancel - returns 404 for nonexistent job
✅ GET /api/celery/stats - returns Celery queue statistics (celery_available: false - fallback mode)
✅ GET /api/celery/workers - returns empty workers list (Redis not running)
✅ Full job lifecycle test (add → start → logs → cancel) - all steps working
✅ All job types supported: prototype, full_build, demo, code_gen, test_suite, asset_pipeline
✅ Worker management: pause/resume working
✅ Modular structure verified: /app/backend/routes/, /app/backend/models/, /app/backend/core/
✅ main.py modular entry point exists
✅ Frontend: SystemVisualization3D renders with pure Three.js (canvas element created)
✅ Frontend: 4 view modes available (radial, spiral, cluster, tree)
✅ Frontend: BuildFarmPanel renders with 'Distributed Build Farm' header
✅ Frontend: Workers section shows 3 workers (Worker-Alpha, Worker-Beta, Worker-Gamma)
✅ Frontend: Queue Prototype button works - shows toast 'Job added to build farm!'
✅ Frontend: No JavaScript console errors
✅ Frontend: Real-time polling for build farm status working
critical_code_review_comments: Pure Three.js implementation in SystemVisualization3D.jsx successfully avoids R3F reconciler issues
Celery integration works in fallback mode (memory queue) when Redis unavailable
Modular structure properly separates routes, models, and core utilities
Build Farm panel has good UX with job type buttons showing estimated times
seed_data_creation: Created test jobs via API for lifecycle testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 3 final tasks verified: 1) server.py modular migration complete with /routes, /models, /core directories and main.py entry point. 2) Celery/Redis distributed workers implemented and working in fallback mode (CELERY_AVAILABLE=false since Redis not running, but endpoints functional). 3) Three.js visualization now uses pure Three.js instead of React Three Fiber - no more reconciler errors. Build Farm Panel with real-time 2s polling working correctly.

rca_of_issue.issue: Previous R3F reconciler error in SystemVisualization3D
rca_of_issue.root_cause: React Three Fiber had reconciler issues during Canvas unmount/remount when switching tabs
rca_of_issue.fix_applied: Rewrote SystemVisualization3D.jsx to use pure Three.js instead of R3F. Manual scene management, camera controls, and raycasting for node selection now all use native Three.js APIs.
rca_of_issue.result: No more R3F errors. Canvas renders properly with 4 view modes (radial, spiral, cluster, tree)
